message:
chore(core/goldens): normalize trailing-newline convention on v1 conformance goldens

Six existing adapter-conformance violation goldens previously ended
with a trailing `\n`. Our golden comparator strips trailing
whitespace before comparison, so the newline difference never caused
test failures — but it did cause every fresh golden write via the
update-tool to produce a one-byte diff, polluting PRs with spurious
changes.

Files:
  adapter_conformance_violations_embedding_invalid_v1.golden.json
  adapter_conformance_violations_index_invalid_v1.golden.json
  adapter_conformance_violations_lifecycle_invalid_v1.golden.json
  adapter_conformance_violations_resource_invalid_v1.golden.json
  adapter_conformance_violations_search_empty_query_v1.golden.json
  adapter_conformance_violations_search_result_count_exceeds_sources_v1.golden.json

Each file is exactly the same JSON array as before, ending at the
closing `]` with no trailing newline — matching the new-goldens
added in the companion commits (filter_fidelity / metadata_redaction
/ privacy_redaction / fsfs explanation payloads) so the entire v1
golden family now has a consistent byte-wise terminator.

Pure cosmetic / byte-layout normalization. No content change — every
violation record inside each array is byte-identical.

files.patch:
@@ -24,4 +24,4 @@
     "field": "embedding.duration_ms",
     "message": "duration_ms should be > 0 for completed telemetry"
   }
-]
+]

-    "message": "load_avg_1m -2 must be finite and >= 0"

   },
   {
     "code": "adapter.event.resource.zero_interval",
     "field": "resource.sample.interval_ms",
     "message": "interval_ms must be greater than zero"
   }
-]

\ No newline at end of file

@@ -4,4 +4,4 @@
